title: lupinus perennis subsp. gracilis
intro: Lupinus perennis subsp. gracilis, also known as the narrowleaf lupine, is a perennial plant species belonging to the lupine family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and muscle cramps. The narrowleaf lupine has been found to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in protecting against cell damage and infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies and supplements.
